session_hint: the report generation scheduling approach for the data pipeline
question: What did we ultimately decide on for triggering report generation?
turns: Nightly reports need to run after the ETL finishes loading data into Redshift. Plan: cron job at 02:00 UTC on the ETL EC2 host to kick off the report generator script.
Cron entry added. report-generator.py queries Redshift and uploads CSVs to S3. Tested manually, works correctly.

ETL run time is variable — it finishes anywhere between 01:45 and 03:30 depending on upstream data volume. Cron at 02:00 races it and we've had three incidents of reports being generated from half-loaded data.

Changed our minds on cron. Switching to event-driven triggers: ETL writes a completion marker to an S3 prefix on success, which fires an S3 event notification that triggers the report Lambda. Reports only run after ETL is confirmed done. Final decision: event-driven.
